author: Igor Holt
research_references: Liu et al. (2026) Structural Alignment Bias - arXiv:2604.11322

proposition_text: The archive mcp-tool-dissonance-v1.0.0-podman.zip contains exactly 15 files.
The archive is exactly 21,826 bytes (21.3 KB) in size.
supporting_evidence: Package Summary table: Size = 21.3 KB (21,826 bytes)

proposition_text: The Go source code totals exactly 749 lines across all .go files.
supporting_evidence: Package Summary table: Go Code Lines = 749

proposition_text: The container process runs as non-root UID 65532 when deployed via any of the four documented methods.
supporting_evidence: Security Features: Rootless - Runs as non-root user (UID 65532)

proposition_text: The final distroless container image contains no shell binary (sh, bash, dash) and no package manager (apt, apk, yum, dnf).
supporting_evidence: Security Features: Distroless - No shell, no package manager in final image

proposition_text: The container cannot escalate privileges at runtime — NoNewPrivileges is enforced at the OCI spec level.
supporting_evidence: Security Features: NoNewPrivileges - Cannot escalate privileges

proposition_text: All Linux capabilities are dropped from the container; DropAllCapabilities is set, leaving effective capability set = empty.
supporting_evidence: Security Features: DropAllCapabilities - Minimal attack surface

proposition_text: Podman auto-update detects and applies image updates when the io.containers.autoupdate=registry label is present in the container spec.
supporting_evidence: Security Features: Auto-updates - podman auto-update compatible

proposition_text: All four deployment methods (manual podman build, deploy-podman.sh, podman-compose, systemd Quadlet) produce a running, healthy container.
The Quadlet unit (mcp-dissonance.container) integrates with systemd --user, enabling service start/stop/status via systemctl --user.
supporting_evidence: File table: mcp-dissonance.container - Quadlet systemd integration

proposition_text: The DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17784838 and ORCID 0009-0008-8389-1297 attribution block is embedded in every file in the archive.
This package is Part 3 of the LID-LIFT Technical Suite (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17784144) — the parent suite record is resolvable and lists this package.
The references Liu et al. (2026) arXiv:2604.11322 and Basu (2026) arXiv:2604.11288 are resolvable and describe Structural Alignment Bias and Transactional Attention respectively.
Podman operates fully daemonless — no persistent background process (podman socket or service) is required for container lifecycle management.
supporting_evidence: Security Features: Daemonless - No persistent background process

proposition_text: The Containerfile builds successfully from scratch using 'podman build -f Containerfile' with a distroless base image and produces a functional Go binary container.
supporting_evidence: File table: Containerfile - Podman-native build (distroless base, non-root)

hypothesis: Unzipping the archive yields exactly 15 files; archive byte size is 21826; 'wc -l' across all .go files sums to 749.

hardware: Any x86_64 host with 1GB free disk

procedure: Step 1: Download mcp-tool-dissonance-v1.0.0-podman.zip from DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17784838
Step 2: stat the zip file and record size in bytes
Step 3: unzip -l to enumerate file count
Step 4: unzip -d /tmp/mcp-dissonance mcp-tool-dissonance-v1.0.0-podman.zip
Step 5: find /tmp/mcp-dissonance -type f | wc -l (verify == 15)
Step 6: find /tmp/mcp-dissonance -name '*.go' -exec wc -l {} + | tail -1 (verify sum == 749)
Step 7: Record pass/fail per criterion
confounders_controls: Use binary-mode stat, not filesystem reporting which may differ by OS
Count only files (not directories) in unzip output
Exclude blank lines from wc -l if spec is ambiguous — document interpretation

hypothesis: Running container shows UID 65532, no shell binaries, NoNewPrivileges=1 in /proc/self/status, and empty effective capability set.
procedure: Step 1: podman build -f Containerfile -t mcp-tool-dissonance:test .
Step 2: podman run --rm -d --name mcp-sec-test mcp-tool-dissonance:test sleep 60
Step 3: podman exec mcp-sec-test id — assert uid=65532
Step 4: podman exec mcp-sec-test sh 2>&1 — assert 'OCI runtime exec failed' or similar (no shell)
Step 5: podman exec mcp-sec-test bash 2>&1 — assert failure
Step 6: podman inspect mcp-sec-test --format '{{.HostConfig.SecurityOpt}}' — assert no-new-privileges present
Step 7: podman inspect mcp-sec-test --format '{{.HostConfig.CapDrop}}' — assert 'ALL' in list
Step 8: podman inspect mcp-sec-test --format '{{.HostConfig.CapAdd}}' — assert empty list
Step 9: podman exec mcp-sec-test cat /proc/self/status | grep NoNewPrivs — assert NoNewPrivs: 1
Step 10: Record all results; podman stop mcp-sec-test
confounders_controls: Distroless images may still have a /bin directory — test specifically for executable shell binary, not path existence
NoNewPrivileges enforcement varies by Podman version; document Podman version in results
Test on both rootless and rootful modes if both are claimed

hypothesis: Each of the 4 deployment methods produces a container that passes a health check within 60s.
procedure: METHOD 1 — Manual Build: podman build -f Containerfile -t mcp-tool-dissonance:1.0.0 . && podman run --rm mcp-tool-dissonance:1.0.0 -- health check; assert exit 0
METHOD 2 — Deploy Script: chmod +x deploy-podman.sh && ./deploy-podman.sh; assert exit 0 and container running
METHOD 3 — Compose: podman-compose up -d; assert all services show 'Up'; podman-compose down

METHOD 4 — Systemd: mkdir -p ~/.config/containers/systemd && cp mcp-dissonance.container ~/.config/containers/systemd/; systemctl --user daemon-reload; systemctl --user start mcp-dissonance; sleep 5; systemctl --user is-active mcp-dissonance; assert 'active'; systemctl --user stop mcp-dissonance
Record pass/fail per method
confounders_controls: Systemd Quadlet requires systemd user session — verify loginctl enable-linger is set
podman-compose version may affect compose YAML parsing — document version
Deploy script may assume specific Podman socket path — test in clean environment

hypothesis: No podman socket daemon is required; auto-update label triggers image refresh; systemctl --user controls Quadlet lifecycle.

hardware: Linux host with Podman 4.x+, systemd user session

procedure: DAEMONLESS: systemctl --user stop podman.socket || true; podman run --rm mcp-tool-dissonance:1.0.0 echo 'ok'; assert success (proves daemonless)
AUTO-UPDATE: podman inspect mcp-tool-dissonance:1.0.0 --format '{{index .Labels "io.containers.autoupdate"}}'; assert value == 'registry'

QUADLET: verify /usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator exists (Quadlet generator); cp mcp-dissonance.container ~/.config/containers/systemd/; systemctl --user daemon-reload; systemctl --user list-unit-files | grep mcp-dissonance; assert unit present
Lifecycle: systemctl --user start mcp-dissonance; systemctl --user status mcp-dissonance; assert ActiveState=active; systemctl --user stop mcp-dissonance; assert ActiveState=inactive
confounders_controls: Some distros have podman socket enabled by default — explicitly disable before daemonless test
Auto-update label is necessary but not sufficient — actual registry pull requires network access; label check is sufficient for this test
Quadlet generator path may differ between Podman 4.4 and 4.6+

hypothesis: Every file in the archive contains the copyright block with ORCID and DOI; both DOIs resolve to valid Zenodo records.
procedure: Step 1: Unzip archive to /tmp/mcp-dissonance
Step 2: for each file in archive: grep -l 'Igor Holt' — assert match in ALL 15 files
Step 3: grep -rl 'ORCID: 0009-0008-8389-1297' /tmp/mcp-dissonance — assert all 15 files
Step 4: grep -rl '10.5281/zenodo.17784838' /tmp/mcp-dissonance — assert all 15 files
Step 5: curl -sI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17784838 — assert HTTP 302/200 redirect to Zenodo
Step 6: curl -sI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17784144 — assert HTTP 302/200 redirect to Zenodo (parent suite)
Step 7: Record attribution_coverage = files_with_attribution / total_files
confounders_controls: Binary files (compiled artifacts if any) may not embed text — check Containerfile spec for whether pre-built binaries are included
DOI resolution may be rate-limited — cache results
Some files may embed attribution in comments vs. headers — document format per file type

hypothesis: arXiv:2604.11322 resolves to a paper describing Structural Alignment Bias; arXiv:2604.11288 resolves to a paper describing Transactional Attention.
procedure: Step 1: curl https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.11322 — assert HTTP 200
Step 2: Extract title and abstract — verify title/abstract contains 'structural alignment' or 'alignment bias'
Step 3: curl https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.11288 — assert HTTP 200
Step 4: Extract title and abstract — verify title/abstract contains 'transactional attention' or 'attention'
Step 5: Check author list on both papers — Liu et al. and Basu respectively
Step 6: Verify publication year aligns with 2026 claim
Step 7: Record title, authors, year for both papers in results artifact
confounders_controls: arXiv papers may be preprints — content alignment is assessed against abstract, not peer-reviewed claims
Year mismatch (2026 claim vs actual submission date) should be flagged as anomaly, not failure
Author name matching should be fuzzy (first author surname)

hypothesis: A naive replicator with only the README-PODMAN.md can follow instructions to build, deploy, and verify security posture — no prior knowledge required.

hardware: Fresh Ubuntu 22.04 LTS VM (no prior Podman installation)
software: Base Ubuntu 22.04 only — no pre-installed tools beyond apt

procedure: Step 0: Snapshot VM state for reproducibility
Step 1: Install Podman per Ubuntu docs (apt install podman) — record version
Step 2: Download archive via DOI — record retrieval time
Step 3: Follow ONLY README-PODMAN.md instructions (no deviation)
Step 4: Execute 'podman build -f Containerfile -t mcp-tool-dissonance:1.0.0 .'
Step 5: Run TEST_002 security posture checks
Step 6: Run TEST_001 integrity checks
Step 7: Record total time-to-verified; flag any README ambiguities found
confounders_controls: VM must be network-isolated except for DOI resolution and base image pull
Document Ubuntu Podman package version — older repos may ship Podman 3.x which lacks Quadlet
Do not use cached images — force fresh pull

system_prompt: You are a precision file-system verification agent. Your sole function is to validate byte-exact and count-exact claims about software archives. You do not infer or estimate — you measure and report.
instructions: Download mcp-tool-dissonance-v1.0.0-podman.zip via DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17784838
Run: stat --format='%s' mcp-tool-dissonance-v1.0.0-podman.zip — record byte size
Run: unzip -l mcp-tool-dissonance-v1.0.0-podman.zip | tail -1 — record file count
Unzip to /tmp/mcp-dissonance-test/
Run: find /tmp/mcp-dissonance-test/ -type f -name '*.go' | xargs wc -l | tail -1 — record total lines
Assert: bytes == 21826, files == 15, lines == 749
Write test_results_001.json with pass/fail per criterion and actual measured values
Write logs/decision_log_001.csv with this agent's decisions
success_criteria: All three numeric assertions pass AND artifacts written
failure_handling: Log exact measured value vs. claimed value; flag as PROP_001/002/003 violation in constraint_violations.json; continue with other agents

system_prompt: You are a container security verification agent specializing in OCI/Podman security posture. You verify runtime security claims using podman inspect, exec, and /proc filesystem queries. You never skip a security check and always document the exact command output.
instructions: Build container: podman build -f Containerfile -t mcp-tool-dissonance:sec-test . 2>&1 | tee logs/build_002.log
Run container detached: podman run -d --name mcp-sec-test mcp-tool-dissonance:sec-test sleep 120
Execute UID check: podman exec mcp-sec-test id 2>&1 | tee results/uid_check.txt
Execute shell check: podman exec mcp-sec-test sh 2>&1 | tee results/shell_sh.txt; podman exec mcp-sec-test bash 2>&1 | tee results/shell_bash.txt
Dump inspect: podman inspect mcp-sec-test > results/podman_inspect_dump.json
Check NoNewPrivs: podman exec mcp-sec-test cat /proc/self/status | grep NoNewPrivs | tee results/nonewprivs.txt
Stop container: podman stop mcp-sec-test && podman rm mcp-sec-test
Evaluate all checks against PROP_004-007 acceptance criteria
Write test_results_002.json and decision_log_002.csv
success_criteria: UID == 65532, shell inaccessible, NoNewPrivs == 1, CapDrop includes ALL, CapAdd empty
failure_handling: Record exact failure with command output; log to constraint_violations.json with severity='error' for any security claim failure; do not suppress output

system_prompt: You are a deployment validation agent. You execute each documented deployment method in isolation, verify it produces a running container, and record exact pass/fail with timing. You treat each method as independent — failure of one does not skip others.
instructions: Ensure clean state before each method: podman rm -f $(podman ps -aq) 2>/dev/null || true
METHOD 1: time podman build -f Containerfile -t mcp-tool-dissonance:1.0.0 . 2>&1 | tee logs/method1.log; run container and verify health
METHOD 2: chmod +x deploy-podman.sh; time ./deploy-podman.sh 2>&1 | tee logs/method2.log; verify container running
METHOD 3: time podman-compose up -d 2>&1 | tee logs/method3.log; podman ps; podman-compose down

METHOD 4: mkdir -p ~/.config/containers/systemd; cp mcp-dissonance.container ~/.config/containers/systemd/; systemctl --user daemon-reload; systemctl --user start mcp-dissonance; sleep 5; systemctl --user is-active mcp-dissonance | tee logs/method4.log; systemctl --user stop mcp-dissonance
Write test_results_003.json with boolean result and duration per method
Write decision_log_003.csv
success_criteria: All 4 methods produce running container AND pass health check
failure_handling: Document exact error output per method; continue with remaining methods; flag partial success

system_prompt: You are a research artifact verification agent. You validate DOI resolution, attribution embedding, and bibliographic accuracy. You use HTTP HEAD requests for DOI resolution and grep for attribution scanning. You are the final authority on whether published claims match verifiable external records.
instructions: ATTRIBUTION: for f in $(find /tmp/mcp-dissonance -type f); do grep -l 'Igor Holt' $f && grep -l '0009-0008-8389-1297' $f && grep -l '10.5281/zenodo.17784838' $f; done; count files matching ALL three patterns
DOI PRIMARY: curl -sI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17784838 | head -5 | tee results/doi_primary.txt; assert HTTP 302 or 200
DOI PARENT: curl -sI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17784144 | head -5 | tee results/doi_parent.txt; assert HTTP 302 or 200

ARXIV 1: curl -s https://export.arxiv.org/abs/2604.11322 | python3 -c "import sys,re; d=sys.stdin.read(); print(re.findall(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', d)[:2])" | tee results/arxiv_1.txt; verify title contains alignment-related terms

ARXIV 2: curl -s https://export.arxiv.org/abs/2604.11288 | python3 -c "import sys,re; d=sys.stdin.read(); print(re.findall(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', d)[:2])" | tee results/arxiv_2.txt; verify title contains attention-related terms
Write test_results_005.json and test_results_006.json
Write decision_log_004.csv
success_criteria: All 15 files attributed, both DOIs resolve, both arXiv IDs resolve with content-aligned titles
failure_handling: Partial pass allowed — flag individual failures per PROP_ID; DOI non-resolution is critical failure (severity=critical)

system_prompt: You are the final-stage integration test agent. You simulate a naive replicator following only README-PODMAN.md. You also verify Podman-native features. You are time-aware — record wall-clock duration for each phase. Flag every point of ambiguity in the README as a structured finding.
instructions: Provision clean Ubuntu 22.04 environment (VM or container-in-container if permitted)
Install Podman: sudo apt-get update && apt-get install -y podman 2>&1 | tee logs/install.log; podman --version | tee results/podman_version.txt
Follow README-PODMAN.md step-by-step — record each command and output
DAEMONLESS CHECK: systemctl --user stop podman.socket 2>/dev/null; podman run --rm mcp-tool-dissonance:1.0.0 echo daemonless-ok
AUTOUPDATE LABEL: podman inspect mcp-tool-dissonance:1.0.0 --format '{{index .Labels "io.containers.autoupdate"}}'
QUADLET CHECK: ls ~/.config/containers/systemd/ | grep mcp-dissonance; systemctl --user list-unit-files | grep mcp-dissonance
Record total elapsed time; list all README ambiguities as structured objects {step, ambiguity_text, severity}
Write e2e_replication_results.json; write readme_ambiguity_log.txt
success_criteria: Build succeeds from README alone, security posture verified, time_to_verified < 30 minutes, readme_ambiguities <= 2
failure_handling: Any README step that fails is a documentation defect — log as constraint_violation with violated_constraint='documentation_accuracy'; continue to next step

title: MCP Tool Dissonance v1.0.0 — Podman Edition Test Dashboard

compliance_mapping.purpose: Energy consumption attribution per test agent execution

notes: Telemetry is optional for static analysis tests (TEST_001, TEST_005, TEST_006) but mandatory for container execution tests (TEST_002, TEST_003, TEST_004, TEST_007)
Each agent writes its own decision_log_{N}.csv; orchestrator merges into decision_log.csv

compliance_mapping.purpose: Failure mode logging for all 15 propositions

purpose: EU AI Act replicability requirement + Podman platform spec

infrastructure.gpu: Optional — GTX 1650 (Diamond Vault edge) or NVIDIA T4 (Swarm Dispatch cloud)
infrastructure.network: Outbound HTTPS required for DOI/arXiv resolution and registry pulls

outputs_to_generate.purpose: Real-time test queue status for all 7 tests across 15 propositions
Comprehensive testing documentation with EU AI Act compliance mapping
Parquet telemetry traces per container execution test
SHA256-checksummed inventory of all generated artifacts

notes: Phase 1 agents require only network access. Phase 2 requires Podman. Phase 3 (AGENT_005) requires clean VM environment — provision separately.
